pulse: The Energy sector currently trades at a median EV/EBITDA of 11.1x and a median P/E of 17.9x, aligning with the broader market multiples. Notably, Exxon Mobil (XOM) has seen its EV/EBITDA increase to 11.5x, significantly above the sector median, driven by rising oil prices now at six-month highs due to Middle East tensions that impact global supply, as highlighted by multiple recent headlines. Looking ahead, investors should be cautious as high geopolitical risks in the Middle East could sustain upward pressure on energy prices, potentially enhancing margins for key players like XOM while weighing negatively on broader market sentiment as indicated by the recent equity sell-off reported by Meyka.
